scope.focus: correctness, security, data, operations, tests, and docs-vs-reality

requiredLenses.name: correctness-integration
requiredLenses.responsibility: Correctness, edge cases, and integration contracts

requiredLenses.name: security-privacy-authz
requiredLenses.responsibility: Security, privacy, authn/authz, and secret handling

requiredLenses.name: data-migrations-idempotency
requiredLenses.responsibility: Data consistency, persistence, idempotency, and migrations

requiredLenses.name: operations-tests-docs
requiredLenses.responsibility: Operations, startup, observability, tests, and docs-vs-reality

delegationPolicy.spawnWhen: The user explicitly authorizes subagents and the Codex runtime exposes a subagent tool.
delegationPolicy.fallback: Run the same independent lenses locally and report single-agent multi-lens.
delegationPolicy.claimRule: Do not claim multi-agent mode unless real subagent IDs or runtime-visible handles exist.

subagentsMustNot: perform side effects unless explicitly assigned a disjoint write scope
commit, push, publish, deploy, mutate credentials, spend funds, or edit issues/PRs
claim final findings without main-agent verification

evidencePolicy.redaction: Never print raw secrets, tokens, private keys, wallet keys, credential files, or .env values.
evidencePolicy.unknowns: Preserve material unknowns, blocked probes, and coverage gaps.

verificationPolicy.candidateStandard: Every reported finding needs a concrete failure scenario and exact evidence.
verificationPolicy.verificationStandard: The main agent must reproduce, refute, or bound high-impact candidates before final reporting.
